sourceMovie.title: Mr. Vampire
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Mr. Vampire for the same reason it broke through in 1985: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ricky Lau keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mr. Vampire clicked for you, Resident Evil: Apocalypse (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Shaolin Soccer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mr. Vampire clicked for you, Shaolin Soccer (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mr. Vampire?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil: Apocalypse, Shaolin Soccer, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles—each shares DNA with Mr. Vampire while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mr. Vampire?
faq.answer: Mr. Vampire (1985) is best described as Action, Comedy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mr. Vampire part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Mr. Vampire Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
